text.title: OpenAPI Example Authoring
text.summary: Rules for embedding request/response examples in OpenAPI specs and FastAPI app metadata.

rule.statement: Set title, description (with auth instructions and rate limits), and version in the FastAPI app constructor to populate Swagger UI and Redoc automatically.
rule.rationale: FastAPI generates OpenAPI from app metadata. Setting it once populates both /docs and /redoc with no extra work.

example.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI
app = FastAPI( title="User Management API", description=( "## Authentication
" "All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

" "## Rate Limits
" "Free tier: 100 req/hour. Pro tier: 10,000 req/hour." ), version="1.0.0", docs_url="/docs", redoc_url="/redoc", )

rule.statement: Add named examples under requestBody and responses for every endpoint that has non-trivial input or output variants.
rule.rationale: Named examples appear in Swagger UI's dropdown, letting developers try each scenario without constructing the body manually.

antipattern.description: No examples in the OpenAPI spec — only schema definitions.
antipattern.reason: Schemas describe shape; examples demonstrate real values. Without examples, Swagger UI generates synthetic data that may not reflect valid inputs.

antipattern.description: Examples that are not tested against the actual API.
antipattern.reason: Untested examples drift from implementation. A consumer who follows the example and gets an error loses trust in all docs.
